theme: Every corner of the room should earn its pixels -- name it, color it, give it stakes
visual_observations: Desktop (1280x800): Vibe is Crushing It, Sunday March 8. The vibe-aura border from review 47 is live -- the office floor glows purple. Nameplate-pop CSS is confirmed shipped. The lower ~40% of the office floor is the story: conference table at 50%/62% sits empty with 6 chairs and zero people, zero label -- just a circle of silence in the visual center of the floor. Bottom-right corner has the vibe-weather emoji floating at 78% opacity with a gentle rotating animation but zero text label -- reads as debug art, not a weather station. Worker desks in the top half each emit tap/clack/click floating keystrokes but all in identical rgba(255,255,255,0.25) grey -- four people, four role colors, zero visual distinction in their typing. Fern the ficus sits at right:87% top:72% -- recognizable foliage shape but no label, no stage indicator, completely silent about what she tracks. The footer renders the vibe-reactive tagline with footer-tag-in animation -- but the tagline looks entirely static; nothing indicates there are 5 different lines or that clicking anything would cycle them. The COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT CSS ::after label is a perfect model of a detail that rewards attention -- more of the floor needs this treatment. Mobile (375x812): Same state, scaled down.

ideas.title: Desk Keystrokes Inherit Role Color
ideas.description: In Office.css line 1826, .desk-keystrokes span has color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25) -- a single neutral grey for every worker's typing floaters. But each working .desk already has --role-color set inline in Office.jsx (~line 1046): style={{ '--role-color': ROLE_COLORS[occ.worker.role] }}. Fix: one CSS change -- replace color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25) with color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--role-color, rgba(255,255,255,0.25)) 35%, transparent). Nothing else. WHY: Right now all four working desks emit identical ghost-grey tap/clack/click clouds. With role-color, Carlos types in blue, Maya in purple, Dave in orange, Sofia in green -- the floor becomes visually polyphonic and you can identify who is typing without reading nameplates. The --role-color variable is already set and waiting; this just puts it to work.

ideas.title: Conference Table Has a Voice When Empty
ideas.description: The ConferenceTable component (Office.jsx line 676) renders a gorgeous vibe-reactive SVG table -- color-coded stroke, red glow on on-fire, coffee emoji on slow-day -- but conf-meeting-badge only appears at meetingWorkers.length >= 2 (Office.css line 1977), which effectively never fires. The table is visually prominent (104x104px at left: 50%, top: 62%) and has zero copy in its default state. Fix: Inside ConferenceTable, below the SVG, add a conditional div.conf-ambient-label when meetingWorkers.length === 0. Content per vibe: crushing -> 'everyone is at their desk', on-fire -> 'no meetings. fix it.', in-flow -> 'reserve the table', slow-day -> 'open office hours', after-hours -> 'locked up'. CSS: .conf-ambient-label { position: absolute; top: 108%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.5rem; color: var(--text-muted); white-space: nowrap; opacity: 0.45; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }. WHY: The conference table occupies the exact visual center of the lower floor in every screenshot. It currently has no copy, no ambient state, no label. Adding 5 vibe-specific captions turns the most prominent empty element into a living part of the office -- the room status board, not just furniture.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Gets a Click-to-Cycle Mechanic
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1204 renders FOOTER_TAGLINES[teamVibe.key] -- 5 brilliant vibe-reactive lines that animate in via .footer-tagline animation. The problem: they look static. 'productivity charts are going vertical. do not look down.' reads as a permanent tagline. Users never discover the other 4. Fix in App.jsx: Add const [quoteBonus, setQuoteBonus] = useState(0). Define BONUS_TAGLINES array of 8 additional Bloberto one-liners (e.g. 'technically it deploys if you squint', 'sprint velocity is just vibes with a spreadsheet', 'the standup is 15 minutes. the anxiety is forever'). Wrap the footer-tagline span with onClick={() => setQuoteBonus(b => b + 1)}, title='tap for another thought', cursor: pointer. On click it alternates between the current vibe tagline and BONUS_TAGLINES[quoteBonus % n]. Add a tiny dot-counter span after the quote: opacity 0.3, font-size 0.7em, showing the cycle index. WHY: The footer is in full view in every single screenshot. Making the quote clickable rewards curiosity, signals Bloberto is alive and opinionated, and adds a small ritual to every visit. The footer-tag-in animation already exists -- click just adds the trigger.

ideas.title: Vibe Weather Station Gets a Caption
ideas.description: The .vibe-weather div (Office.jsx ~line 1235, Office.css line 1695) renders a lone animated emoji at right: 3%, bottom: 26% with opacity: 0.78. There is nothing else -- no label, no context. In the screenshot it reads as a floating emoji with no relationship to the rest of the floor. Fix: In Office.jsx, add a span.vibe-weather__label as a child of the vibe-weather div, immediately after the emoji. Labels per vibe: crushing -> 'CLEAR', on-fire -> 'STORM', in-flow -> 'OVERCAST', slow-day -> 'DRIZZLE', after-hours -> 'NIGHT'. In Office.css add: .vibe-weather__label { display: block; font-size: 0.42rem; text-align: center; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; margin-top: 2px; }. WHY: The weather metaphor is a second, quieter layer of vibe expression that is completely legible as decoration right now. A 5-character ALLCAPS label like CLEAR or STORM below the emoji completes the metaphor and mirrors the exact same understated-detail energy as the TRIPLE SHOT coffee label. The coffee corner is proof this pattern works; apply it to the weather too.

ideas.title: Fern Has a Hover Stage Badge
ideas.description: Fern the office ficus (Office.jsx lines 1201-1212) has title='Fern the office ficus' -- a browser tooltip -- but no visible UI element reveals her current stage or growth progress. plantStage cycles through ['seedling','sprout','small-plant','leafy','blooming'] based on Math.floor(vibeStreak / 5). plantMilestone boolean (line 852) is already tracked. Fix: Inside .office-plant--ficus div, add a div.ficus-badge child: the stage emoji (seedling=seedling emoji, sprout=herb emoji, small-plant=potted plant emoji, leafy=tree emoji, blooming=cherry blossom emoji) followed by '{plantStage} . Fern'. Pass vibeStreak as a prop (it already arrives on the Office component at line 838). CSS: .ficus-badge { position: absolute; bottom: -14px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.42rem; color: var(--text-muted); white-space: nowrap; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.2s; } -- .office-plant--ficus:hover .ficus-badge { opacity: 0.75; } -- .office-plant--ficus[data-milestone='true'] .ficus-badge { opacity: 0.9; } so the badge stays visible during the level-up moment. WHY: Fern is the only persistent achievement metric in the office -- the team's sustained excellence made tangible. Without any label she is just a decorative plant. The hover badge creates a ritual: let me check on Fern. It costs zero extra UI space and zero performance impact.

ideas.title: Weekend Warriors Mode
ideas.description: The header already shows 'Sunday, Mar 8' -- the product is fully aware of the real day. In both screenshots, 5 workers are active on a Sunday at Crushing It vibe. This specific, real absurdity is completely ignored by the office. Fix: In Office.jsx, add const isWeekend = useMemo(() => { const d = new Date().getDay(); return d === 0 || d === 6; }, []). When isWeekend && (vibe === 'crushing' || vibe === 'on-fire'), render a div.weekend-warriors-badge inside .office-floor positioned top: 1.5%, right: 3% (adjacent to the full-sync banner logic at line 976). Badge text: 'WEEKEND WARRIORS'. CSS: font-size: 0.48rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #fbbf24; border: 1px solid rgba(245,158,11,0.4); background: rgba(245,158,11,0.1); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 6px; animation: badge-pop 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both. Also: add a WEEKEND_QUOTES array gated by isWeekend in the Bloberto speech bubble pool: ['no days off. just different background music.', 'is it a weekend if you love what you do? asking for a lawyer.', 'the build server does not know it is Sunday. we do not correct it.', 'technically this is rest. rest from the other 6 days of this.']. WHY: The product shows the real date and claims to be a live monitor. A team crushing it on a Sunday is a specific emotional fact -- equal parts impressive and alarming. Acknowledging it makes Bloberto feel genuinely sentient, not just data-aware. One useMemo, one conditional render, fires 2 out of 7 days -- but on those days it is the most on-brand thing in the entire office.
